---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: true
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-section container">
  <div class="row align-items-center">
    <div class="col-md-8">
      <p class="hero-name">Data with a Plot</p>
      <p class="hero-tagline">Lindsey E. Wylie, J.D., Ph.D.</p>
      <div class="hero-pills">
        <span class="hero-pill">Applied Research</span>
        <span class="hero-pill">Program Evaluation</span>
        <span class="hero-pill">Measurement & Design</span>
        <span class="hero-pill">Storytelling with Data</span>
      </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Featured Project {.section-heading}

::::: project-grid
:::: project-card
[Data Storytelling]{.project-card-tag}

<p class="project-card-title">

[A Periodic Table of Hip Hop Artists]{.project-card-title}

</p>

<p class="project-card-desc">

[A dataset built to teach measurement validity, sampling bias,
uncertainty communication, and visualization design — using a subject
matter executives actually argue about.]{.project-card-desc}

</p>

::: project-card-meta
R / Quarto Interactive Teaching module
:::

<br>
<a href="hiphop/hiphop_periodic_table.html" style="font-weight:600; color:#E98973;">
Explore the periodic table → </a>
::::
:::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## About {#about .section-heading}

:::::: bio-block
::: bio-avatar-placeholder
LEW
:::

:::: {}
My work explores intersection of law, data, and decision-making. My
non-work work focuses on making rigorous analytic thinking accessible —
through unexpected data sets, practical methodology,and data
visualizations that tell stories.

::: credential-badges
[PhD, Social Psychology, certificate in Research Methods and Data
Analysis]{.badge-item} [JD, concentration in Health Law]{.badge-item}
[Senior Research Associate / National Center for State
Courts]{.badge-item}
:::

I am particularly interested in the unintended consequences of legal
decisions and legal interventions.
::::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What I Do {#whatIdo .section-heading}

:::::: columns
:::::: {.columns-title}
Teaching
::::::
Executive and graduate education in data storytelling, measurement
design, and evidence-based decision-making. I teach using datasets with documented flaws — because clean pedagogical data teaches the wrong
lessons.
::::::

:::::: columns
::: {.columns-title}
Research
:::
Applied work within law, psychology, and data storytelling. Particular
interest in construct validity, uncertainty quantification, and the
politics of how metrics get built and used.
::::::

:::::: columns
::: {.columns-title}
Projects
:::
Independent projects that translate methodological ideas into
interactive, shareable artifacts. The hip-hop table is one.
::::::
------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {style="text-align: center; padding: 3rem 0 1rem; color: #6b7280; font-size: 0.9rem;"}
Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) · Hosted on [GitHub
Pages](https://pages.github.com)
:::
